title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Margaret discusses the character of Harriet Spear with Mr. Kachpur. She explains that Harriet was once a gentle and selfless woman, but her personality changed drastically following the death of her husband, George, from brain thrombosis in 1911. Margaret describes the current Harriet as a malicious woman who seeks out perceived evils in others to satisfy a dark passion for condemnation, maintaining a group of followers who join her attacks to avoid becoming her next target.

The conversation shifts to the other victims. Margaret describes Edith Gransbury as a hypocritical egomaniac who condemned others out of a sense of religious duty and fear of divine retribution. In contrast, Margaret views Ada Gransbury as a principled woman who believed in redemption and saving sinners. Margaret notes that Ada had performed unknown harmful deeds, though she refuses to disclose the details until the following day.

Regarding Richard Nigges, Margaret notes he was an influential figure in Gresholm who had previously canceled his engagement to Ada. According to the village doctor, Ambrose Flavoward, Ada never spoke of Nigges after his departure, claiming she would devote herself to God. However, Kachpur reveals that Ada and Nigges were seen having tea together in a London hotel last Thursday, the same night Margaret was sitting alone looking at the cemetery. Margaret speculates that Harriet Spear was also at the hotel and suggests that the meeting between Ada and Nigges involved something significant enough to cross their established boundaries.
